entry.title: Re: Cross model copy from old model (Account base) to New...
entry.content:
Hi @9160ruby 

When cross-model copy from the Account model to the Measure model, the default currency value is copied. There is no way to copy from the "Local currency value" of the Account model to the Measure model. 

The workaround solution is to convert from the default currency value to the Local currency on the measure model. 

  • Create one more Measreu with the Default currency. (ex, USD)
  • Copy from the account model default value to the measure "USD".

    • DATA([d/Measures] = "USD", [d/Accounts] = [Model_Old].[d/Accounts], [d/Date] = [Model_Old].[d/Date])
      = LINK([Model_Old], [d/Version] = %SourceVersion%)

  • Currency conversion from "USD" measure to "LC" measure on the Measure model.  

 

I hope this is helpful to you, and if I have misunderstood anything, please feel free to reach out.

Moonjun

entry.title: While using change tank dip option, customer fields (CUST1, CUST2) are not populated in o4_TIGER
entry.content:
While using change tank dip option, customer fields (CUST1, CUST2) are not populated in o4_TIGER. these fields are populated while creating Tank dip.
the structure ROITANKDIP has these fields however there is no corresponding table that stores these fields

entry.title: Re: Non Standard Allocation in SAC
entry.content:
I don't know why your product 40 is not in the output, but this could be the code for the allocation to work

MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "Amount"

AGGREGATE_DIMENSIONS = [d/COMPCODE], [d/ACCOUNT], [d/COSTCENTER], [d/ITEM_CODE]
AGGREGATE_WRITETO [d/COMPCODE] = "CC01"
AGGREGATE_WRITETO [d/ACCOUNT] = "AAC5"
AGGREGATE_WRITETO [d/COSTCENTER] = "COSTC1"
AGGREGATE_WRITETO [d/ITEM_CODE] = "SL1000"

VARIABLEMEMBER #TOTAL OF [d/AUDITTRAIL]
DATA([d/AUDITTRAIL] = #TOTAL, [d/COLLECTION] = "#", [d/COUNTRY] = "#", [d/BRAND] = "#", [d/GENDER] = "#", [d/PRODUCT] = "#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/AUDITTRAIL] = "DT02") 

DATA([d/AUDITTRAIL] = "DT03") = (RESULTLOOKUP([d/AUDITTRAIL] = "DT02") / RESULTLOOKUP([d/AUDITTRAIL] = #TOTAL, [d/COLLECTION] = "#", [d/COUNTRY] = "#", [d/BRAND] = "#", [d/GENDER] = "#", [d/PRODUCT] = "#")) * RESULTLOOKUP([d/AUDITTRAIL] = "DT01", [d/COLLECTION] = "#", [d/COUNTRY] = "#", [d/BRAND] = "#", [d/GENDER] = "#", [d/PRODUCT] = "#")

entry.title: Automatically export / save update errors and short dumps
entry.content:
Hi,

As auditors keep an eye on missing document numbers, we have to explain these.

We found out they often come from update errors in SM13 (with corresponding short dumps in ST22).

For documentation and analysis we need to be able to access these historically.
As short dumps are reorganized regularly and update errors should be deleted before updates we need another solution.

Is there a way to automatically export update errors and (corresponding) short dumps?

Thank you for feedback & Best regards,

Jonas

entry.title: Re: Could not commit transaction: Error -1 detected during transaction exception in ServiceLayer
entry.content:
We have the same error build, while we try to add user defined tables. This is our payload:

{
"TableName":"<some table name>",
"TableDescription":"<some table description>",
"TableType":"bott_NoObjectAutoIncrement",
"Archivable":"tNO",
"ArchiveDateField":null,
"DisplayMenu":"tYES",
"ApplyAuthorization":"tNO"
}

The first 1 - 5 trials was successful, but now we only become the error message from service layer:
-1116 / Could not commit transaction: Error -1 detected during transaction

We have restarted the service layer service with the SAP Business One Service Layer Controller, but it does not help.

Our system:
SAP Business One 10.0 version for SAP HANA (10.00.280) FP 2502 (64-bit)

Update:
I think it's the table name or the description, because if I use other values for the fields, it works.

When I try to add the table via the SAP client, I get the following error message:
Could not commit transaction: Error -1 detected during transaction 'Dynamic authorization' (CDPM)

entry.title: CAP application not working with HANA Cloud DB after deployment
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a very simple CAP project in BAS with HANA Cloud DB and Fiori Elements with OData v4 (without any service implementation or extensions). I deployed this project on client's BTP subaccount CF space without any errors, but when I click on Create button in Fiori Elements UI, I receive 500 error and the OData service log shows below error:

argument type mismatch: not supported path expression: must be json list for array index access\n-> $[\"DraftUUID\"]\n-> ^\n 

In my understanding, CAP service is not able to create a Draft row in HANA DB when UI sends a POST call with empty object as payload (standard fiori elements stuff).

However, when I downloaded the codebase from client's BAS, imported in my trial account and deployed, it works as it should. The draft gets created successfully in trial account and I see the Object page in create mode. I compared the tables and views created in the HDI container and those are exactly the same.

On client's stack, I have undeployed and deployed this app several times and nothing works. 

 

Can you please help in fixing this issue?

Here is the package.json file:

{
  "name": "papm-poc2",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A simple CAP project.",
  "repository": "<Add your repository here>",
  "license": "UNLICENSED",
  "private": true,
  "dependencies": {
    "@sap/cds": "^8.7.2",
    "express": "^4",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4.4.0",
    "@cap-js/hana": "^1.6.1"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cap-js/cds-types": "^0.9.0",
    "@cap-js/sqlite": "^1",
    "@sap/cds-dk": "^8.7.2",
    "rimraf": "^5.0.5",
    "mbt": "^1.2.29"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "start": "cds-serve",
    "watch-papmpoc2costpool": "cds watch --open papmpoc2costpool/webapp/index.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false",
    "undeploy": "cf undeploy papm-poc2 --delete-services --delete-service-keys --delete-service-brokers",
    "build": "rimraf resources mta_archives && mbt build --mtar archive",
    "deploy": "cf deploy mta_archives/archive.mtar --retries 1"
  },
  "cds": {
    "requires": {
      "connectivity": true,
      "destinations": true,
      "html5-runtime": true,
      "[production]": {
        "db": "hana",
        "auth": "xsuaa"
      }
    },
    "sql": {
      "native_hana_associations": false
    }
  },
  "sapux": [
    "app/papmpoc2costpool"
  ]
}

Here is the mta.yaml file:

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: papm-poc2
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
  - name: papm-poc2-srv
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/srv
    requires:
      - name: papm-poc2-db
      - name: papm-poc2-connectivity
      - name: papm-poc2-destination
      - name: papm-poc2-auth
    provides:
      - name: srv-api
        properties:
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      instances: 1
    build-parameters:
      builder: npm-ci
  - name: papm-poc2-db-deployer
    type: hdb
    path: gen/db
    requires:
      - name: papm-poc2-db
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
  - name: papm-poc2-destinations-deployer
    type: com.sap.application.content
    requires:
      - name: srv-api
      - name: papm-poc2-srv
      - name: papm-poc2-destination
        parameters:
          content-target: true
      - name: papm-poc2-html5-repo-host
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: papm-poc2-repo-host-key
      - name: papm-poc2-auth
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: papm-poc2-auth-key
    parameters:
      content:
        instance:
          destinations:
            # - Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
            #   HTML5.Timeout: 180000
            #   Name: papm-poc2-srv
            #   TokenServiceInstanceName: papm-poc2-auth
            #   TokenServiceKeyName: papm-poc2-auth-key
            #   URL: ~{srv-api/srv-url}
            - Name: papm-poc2-repo-host
              ServiceInstanceName: papm-poc2-html5-service
              ServiceKeyName: papm-poc2-repo-host-key
              sap.cloud.service: com.client.ra.poc2
            - Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
              Name: papm-poc2-auth
              ServiceInstanceName: papm-poc2-auth
              ServiceKeyName: papm-poc2-auth-key
              sap.cloud.service: com.client.ra.poc2
          existing_destinations_policy: update
    build-parameters:
      no-source: true
  - name: papm-poc2-app-deployer
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    requires:
      - name: papm-poc2-html5-repo-host
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - comclientrapapmpoc2costpool.zip
          name: comclientrapapmpoc2costpool
          target-path: resources/
  - name: comclientrapapmpoc2costpool
    type: html5
    path: app/papmpoc2costpool
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms: []
resources:
  - name: papm-poc2-auth
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        tenant-mode: dedicated
        xsappname: papm-poc2-${org}-${space}
      path: ./xs-security.json
      service: xsuaa
      service-plan: application
  - name: papm-poc2-db
    type: com.sap.xs.hdi-container
    parameters:
      service: hana
      service-plan: hdi-shared
  - name: papm-poc2-connectivity
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: connectivity
      service-plan: lite
  - name: papm-poc2-destination
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
                HTML5.ForwardAuthToken: true
                Name: papm-poc2-srv-api
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: ~{srv-api/srv-url}
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com
            existing_destinations_policy: update
      service: destination
      service-plan: lite
    requires:
      - name: srv-api
  - name: papm-poc2-html5-repo-host
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: html5-apps-repo
      service-name: papm-poc2-html5-service
      service-plan: app-host
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm ci
        - npx cds build --production

Br, Ekansh

SAP HANA Cloud SAP Cloud Application Programming Model SAP Fiori Elements

entry.title: How to Prepare for the SAP BTP Solution Architect Certification (with Sample Questions)
entry.content:
In this blog, I’ll share practical tips to help you to prepare and be able to pass the SAP BTP Solution Architect certification (P_BTPA_2408). While the post is especially helpful for those aiming to get certified, it also provides useful insights for SAP developers, architects, project managers, and anyone daily involved in SAP BTP projects or operations.

Being a successful SAP Solution Architect or Enterprise Architect requires a deep understanding of both on-premises and hyperscaler technologies, combined with hands-on experience from real customer projects. On top of that, strong expertise in SAP solutions is essential. Earning the SAP Solution Architect certification is just the beginning of this journey.

In this blog series, I’ll share practical insights to help you prepare and succeed to achieve this goal.

Exam Topics and Focus Areas

To pass the certification you’ll need a score of 68% or higher, SAP outlines the following key areas:

  • Understanding the Intelligent Enterprise and SAP’s reference architecture
  • Familiarity with SAP BTP services, account models, capabilities (including AI), suite qualities, extensibility types, clean core, and migration strategies (greenfield, brownfield)
  • SAP Methodologies: Integration Solution Advisory Methodology (ISA-M), Application Extension Methodology, and Data & Analytics Methodology
  • Identity and access management types, CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model), RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model), and runtime environments
  • DevOps principles, SAP BTP commercial models, and master data replication
  • Recommending solution designs based on requirements and compliance

Key Areas of Study

To gain the knowledge required, focus on the following areas:

  • Enterprise Architect vs. Solution Architect roles
  • SAP BTP Framework and Reference Architectures
  • SAP Discovery Centre, Business Accelerator Hub, and Trust Centre
  • SAP BTP account model (global accounts, directories, subaccounts)
  • BTP Identity and Access Management (IAM)
  • BTP Connectivity and SAP Cloud Connector
  • S/4HANA implementation types: Greenfield, Brownfield, Bluefield
  • Application development frameworks and extensibility
  • Clean Core principles and extensibility models
  • SAP BTP Data and Analytics (SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud)
  • SAP Business Data Cloud
  • SAP Data and Analytics advisory Methodology
  • SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology (ISA-M)
  • SAP Integration solution Capabilities and Services
  • SAP Build Process Automation, and Signavio
  • SAP AI tools like Joule, and Generative AI Hub
  • SAP BTP Commercial Models and BTP estimating costs

Deep Dive: ISA-M in Practice

ISA-M is central to BTP solution architecture and used throughout the integration lifecycle:

  • Phase 1: Assess your integration strategy (e.g., cloud-to-cloud, on-premise-to-cloud)
  • Phase 2: Design the hybrid integration platform by mapping use case patterns to technology
  • Phase 3: Define best practices, blueprints, and guidelines
  • Phase 4: Establish organizational and operational models for enterprise integration

If you study the Learning Hub courses listed below, you'll gain the core knowledge needed for this exam.
For deeper insight into areas you may not work with daily, make sure to check out the advanced learning links shared below as well.
The exam time is fair, and by practicing with BTP trial environments or your organization’s non-production BTP landscape, you’ll be well prepared to pass the exam.

Recommended (Free) Learning Resources

  • Becoming an SAP BTP Solution Architect (main course)
  • Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Delta Content for BTP Solution Architect

Important Reference Links

  • Integration Architecture Guide (ISA-M)
    NoteBookLM Audio Dialog
  • Integration Solution Advisory Methodology PDF Guide
    NoteBookLM Audio Dialog
  • SAP BTP Guideline Framework
  • SAP Application Extension Methodology
  • ISA-M Presentation (PDF)
  • Extension Architecture Guide
  • SAP Data & Analytics Advisory Methodology Overview

Advanced Learning and Practice

  • Administering SAP BTP
  • Create a BTP Trial Account
  • SAP Trust Centre (Security and Compliance)
  • BTP Account Model & Entitlements
  • SAP BTP Admin Guide
  • OpenSAP Video: ISA-M Integration Assessment
  • Integration Suite Assessment Tool
  • Migration Assessment App
  • Setting up SAP Mobile Start with SAP S/4HANA on SAP Build Work Zone
  • Clean Core Three-Tier Extensibility
  • SAP Discovery Centre:  Reference Architectures & Estimators
  • Cost Estimator
  • Cloud Integration Automation Service User Guide

I've put together 15 sample questions to help you prepare for the exam. These questions are a bit more challenging than the ones you'll typically see on the actual test.

1. Which statement is true about SAP trail account in BTP?
a. A BTP trial account includes only 6 GB of memory for applications
b. You are able to move your Proof of Concept from BTP trail to Pay-As-You-Go account
c. A trial account will be removed when a user not logged on after 90 days
d. You need to manually restart of your solution like HANA DB every day

2. Which SAP licensing model In SAP BTP supports: "The total monthly cost would be deducted from the cloud credits balance monthly"?
a. SAP BTPEA
b. Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP
c. Subscription
d. SAP Full User Equivalent (FUE)

3. In which level of BTP account you can add or modify your Identity Authentication Service was assigned?
a. Global account
b. Subaccount
c. Directory
d. All answers above

4. In SAP Road Map, SAP decided to have the traditional Early Watch Report in which solutions or platform?
a. SAP Solution Manager
b. SAP Cloud ALM
c. SAP Early Watch alert Workspace
d. DVM in SAP For Me launchpad

5. According to the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, the Enterprise Architect is responsible for which of the following activities?
a. Definition of integration standards and technology selection guidelines (cross-vendor, hybrid integration platform)
b. Definition of technical integration architectures with a focus on specific integration technologies
c. Definition and communication of patterns, templates, and best practices
d. All of above
Reference: page 51
Link   page 51
6. A customer has BOBJ and BODS in their existing SAP on premises landscape, when they are going to migrate to S/4 HANA private edition which solution they can pick to support a similar functionality? Make sure the solution is aligned with SAP latest future road map.
a. HANA Cloud, Datasphere
b. SAP Analytic Cloud (SAC), SAP S/4 HANA
c. SAP Intelligent Suite, SAP BW cloud
d. SAP Analytic Cloud (SAC), Datasphere

7. Which statement about SAP Clean Core concept is NOT correct?
a. Deploying a new custom code cannot be on core system (by stack).
b. The clean core strategy advocates for separating custom extensions from the core SAP application.
c. Use "Key User Extensibility" functions of S/4HANA for simple extensions.
d. In classic Extensibility: Applicable primarily to on-premises editions, this tier includes traditional customization methods.

8. Your company has S/4 HANA provisioned in National Security Services (NS2) in Azure, for the entry point of external users you as Solution Architect recommending BTP work Zone as an option.
Which answer meets the requirements (as a cost-effective option) for number 1, 2 descriptions in the diagram below:

a. SAP Build Work Zone advanced edition, SAP Connectivity Service
b. SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, SAP Connectivity Service
c. SAP Build Work Zone advanced edition, Cloud Connector
d. SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, Cloud Connector

9. You are acting as a Solution Architect guiding a migration team that is transitioning from an on-premises SAP PI/PO system to the SAP BTP Integration Suite.
Which of the following tools or services should you recommend to the team, in their appropriate sequence, to support this migration effectively?
a.
SAP BTP Migration Assessment
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
SAP Readiness Check
b.
SAP Readiness Check
SAP BTP Migration Assessment
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
c.
SAP Readiness Check
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
SAP BTP Migration Assessment
d.
SAP BTP Integration Assessment
SAP Readiness Check
SAP BTP Migration Assessment

10. Which of the following SAP BTP services are required or typically used by SAP Cloud ALM?
Select all correct answers (3):
a. SAP Alert Notification Service
b. SAP Automation Pilot
c. SAP Cloud Transport Management
d. SAP Build Work Zone
e. SAP Analytic Cloud
f. SAP Conversational AI

11. Which service can provide a rough rate cost for any service you are going to purchase for BTP solution:
a. SAP For Me Legal & cost
b. SAP Cloud estimator
c. SAP BTP Service Catalog
d. SAP BTP Service Estimator

12. A customer is planning to implement Change Request Management (ChaRM) in the cloud after migrating to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition on a hyperscaler (e.g., Azure).
Which two main tools or technologies can they use to manage change and transport processes in this cloud-based environment?
a. SAP Solution Manager, CTS
b. Cloud Transport Management System (cTMS), SAP Cloud ALM
c. SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, SAP Cloud ALM
d. SAP Solution Manager, Cloud Transport Management System (cTMS)

13. During the Explore phase of an SAP S/4HANA implementation using SAP Activate methodology, a customer aims to validate fit-to-standard processes and ensure alignment with future-state operations.
They also want to establish a monitoring and task management structure early to prepare for efficient project execution and operations.
Which of the following combinations of tools and capabilities are best aligned to support these objectives? (Choose two.)
a. SAP Signavio Process Insights – to compare legacy KPIs against SAP standard benchmarks and identify transformation opportunities
b. SAP Cloud ALM – to import and manage business process models, simulate variants, and execute fit-to-standard workshops
c. SAP Signavio Process Manager – to model target processes and support fit-to-standard workshops using process content from SAP Best Practices
d. SAP Cloud ALM – to orchestrate project tasks, manage requirements, and support test case management from the Explore phase onward
e. SAP Solution Manager – to configure business roles and monitor production incidents during the Explore phase

14. In the context of SAP Event Mesh and SAP BTP, which of the following are required to establish secure communication between publishers and subscribers? (Choose two)
a. XSUAA service for OAuth authentication
b. Connectivity service with destination type = HTTP
c. Application router for handling protocol mapping
d. Event Mesh instance bound to each application with service keys
e. Transport Layer Security (TLS) tunnelling via Cloud Connector

15. What are benefits of using the Cloud Foundry environment in SAP BTP for event-driven extensions? (Choose two)
a. Native ABAP integration without additional runtimes
b. Horizontal scalability for stateless microservices
c. Event handling with built-in webhook configuration
d. Support for event consumers written in various languages (e.g., Node.js, Java)
e. Built-in support for SAP GUI transactions

Answer Keys:
1-Answer: d; 2.Answer: a; 3.Answer: b; 4.Answer: d; 5.Answer: a; 6.Answer: d; 7.Answer: a;
8.Answer: d;9.Answer: b; 10.Answer: a, b, c; 11.Answer: d; 12.Answer: b; 13.Answer: c, d; 14.Answer: a, d;
15.Answer: b, d

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: 3 Things You Need to Know About the Update in Email Notifications
entry.content:
Hello SAP community members!

This article is intended as a newsletter for all our SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model (SAP PaPM Cloud SM) users, providing you with the three important information you need to know on the most recent change to email notification services.

Without further ado, let’s get started!

1. What are the updates in email notifications?

  • Email sender – instead of PAPM@sap.com the sender will now be papm@notifications.sap.com
  • Email contents – an update to the email content which will provide a clearer and more user-readable text.

See examples below:

Subscribing or unsubscribing from email notifications:

Activity Status Report (Open, Completed, Rejected, and Approved)

2. What does this update mean to our SAP PaPM Cloud SM solution?

To align with SAP's email handling capabilities, we are now transitioning from the using the current SAP SMTP service to a new solution, ANS (Alert Notification Service).

This change will automatically be applied in your SAP PaPM Cloud SM solution; however, it is important to take note that there are certain regions which will not be supported with this transition due to the absence of a built-in Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP) service.

See Notification Settings help portal for the list of regions.

BUT DO NOT WORRY!

💡 Important: If your tenant is residing in these regions and would need to have an email notification service enabled, simply proceed to number 3.

3. How will I enable email notifications in my tenant if my region is not supported with this update?

For SAP PaPM Cloud SM tenants that are residing in regions that are not supported with this update, you may enable your email notifications by following the steps below:

  • Create Mail Destination in SAP BTP
  • Configure Destination in Standard Model

Did you know that you can still use your own email notification service instead of the default one provided by SAP PaPM Cloud SM? You can configure your own email notification service, which will take priority over the built-in service.

After enabling the email destination in BTP and in Standard Model application, the email sender will remain as, PAPM@sap.com but the email content will be the same. That easy!

I hope this blogpost provided you with enough information that you need to know about the latest email notification changes in SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model.

Thank you for reading!

entry.title: Re: Free Form Payments Process in SAP
entry.content:
Hello Abha,

I believe you have question to following documentation https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/031c345485b84c8c94265be9ef61d3a8/a0dc70f8489b444886961046bf04b106.html?q=process%20free%20form%20payments&locale=en-US.

There is the My Free Form Payments and Process Free Form Payments app. You can create, post, reverse and release 3 types of payment requests:
Free Form Payments,
Payments for Costumers,
Payments for Suppliers.

When creating the Free Form Payments, you can use Payee's IBAN or bank details (country, bank, account) or bank alias. 

Payments are allowed for all currencies (only Swiss QR-Bills are supported in CHF and EUR).

One-time customers/suppliers are supported.

The Automatic Payment Program is part of the process it is called when you Release the payment request.

In the mentioned documentation you can find the following business example:

  1. While working in an app, an accounts payable account determines the need to create a payment request. The accountant creates a free form payment request in My Free Form Payments.

  2. Later, the accountant decides to change the details of the request. They go to the My Free Form Payments app and find the request they have created, make the changes, then notify the cash specialist of the request.

  3. The cash specialist goes to the Process Free Form Payments app and reviews the request, posts it, and releases it. The request is incorporated into a payment batch and enters the approval process.

  4. An accounting manager uses the Monitor Payments app to review the batch containing the payment request to determine the status of the batch in the approval process.

  5. An accounting manager uses the Approve Bank Payments app to approve the payment batch containing the payment request.

This is a nice demonstration of the payment request flow/lifecycle.

Kind regards
Petr Benes

entry.title: Re: Delete Employee From SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi @salimo ,

Which purge job are you using? If you are using Master Data Purge, you can uncheck those checkboxes and it should delete the person successfully, as long as they have an inactive status. There is also an option to Ignore data retention time configuration for this purge request, so use that if needed also. 

If you are using another purge job and can't use these options, you should be able to run a report showing what forms the person owns or are part of. Delete any forms they own, and remove them from forms they are on, and hopefully that will clear the way to purge them. 

Kind regards,
Cheryl Donnelly - SAP SuccessFactors Preferred Success Platform Functional Specialist

entry.title: Re: Unable to Display Physically Deleted WBS Elements
entry.content:
Hi Mohamed El Amine AOURAGH.

There is a configuration that determines whether a project version is saved based on the status. WBS elements are stored in table VSPRPS_CN. When a WBS element that already has a "status-dependent version" saved in VSPRPS_CN is deleted, it is only removed from table PRPS, while its version remains in VSPRPS_CN.

If you try to create a new WBS element using the same code, the system attempts to save a status-dependent version for it. However, this causes an error because an entry with that code already exists in VSPRPS_CN.

Refer to SAP Note 529634 - Behavior of status-dependent versions for more details.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: SAC’s Time Hierarchy limitation with SuccessFactors Live OData Connection
entry.content:
Summary

We need to configure a month-, quarter-, and year-level headcount and attrition time-series analyses over a history in SAC, based on the SuccessFactors fields Date of Joining and Last Working Date. However, under a live OData connection from SuccessFactors, SAC treats those fields as plain dimensions—because OData maps ABAP’s date types to Edm.DateTime and SAC’s live connector doesn’t infer the SAC “Date” semantic type—which prevents SAC’s native time hierarchies (Year → Quarter → Month → Week → Day) from being available. The following post outlines the business context, the precise problem face : 

1. Business Scenario

To build time-series analyses for headcount and attrition metrics leveraging live data from SAP SuccessFactors. The end users require:

  • Headcount snapshots of total active employees at each period end, and

  • Attrition rates calculated as leavers during the period divided by average headcount,
    broken down by calendar year, quarter, month, and week, with drill-down capability and filters by department, region, job family, and other organizational dimensions. 

This must operate entirely over the existing live OData connection—we cannot import or replicate data to any other platform (BW, HANA, Datasphere or WFA). 

2. Problem Statement

Under SAC’s live OData connection to SuccessFactors, fields such as Date of Joining and Last Working Date arrive as Edm.DateTime properties in the OData service and are ingested by SAC as generic dimensions without any date semantics. As a result:

  • SAC’s predefined calendar hierarchies (Year → Quarter → Month → Week → Day) are not generated for these dimensions, because SAC only auto-creates them on dimensions explicitly tagged with the SAC Date semantic type in import models. 

  • Model-level configuration is read-only in a live model, so you cannot reassign semantic types or define hierarchies for these fields within SAC. 

  • Native drill-down functionality in charts—users clicking from Year down to Month or Week—is unavailable for these date attributes, blocking standard time-series analytics. 

I’m looking for suggestions or ideas on how to tackle a specific requirement using a live OData-only SAC model. If anyone knows of any workarounds, lesser-known features, or upcoming updates that might help in this scenario, I’d really appreciate it. Thanks!

MEMBERSET [d/ACCOUNT].[p/ITEM_CODE] = "SL1000"

entry.title: PIR Consumption & Reduction
entry.content:
Intro

Most asked question in SAP Interviews and Exams. May all know but let us compile it.

Main part.

Take reference from these Q&A1, Q&A2

PIR Consumption: Sales order consume PIR quantity in MD04 screen. No table updated in MD61. See the below difference.

PIR Reduction: GI or GR reduces PIR quantity in MD61. Table will be updated in MD61 and increases the withdrawal quantity automaticaly in background.

 

Planning StrategyPIR Consumption (When)PIR Reduction (When)Mandatory "Mixed MRP"
10No Consumption of PIR against incoming sales orderWhen goods issue or delivery done for sale order (601 Movement Type-PGI) according to consumption mode & period maintained in material masterNA
11No Consumption of PIR against incoming sales orderWhen GR (101 movement type) is done for production order according to consumption mode & period maintained in material masterYES. Mixed MRP = 2.
20No PIR, So No ConsumptionNOT APPLICABLENA
40When sales order is enteredWhen delivery (PGI) is posted against the sale order (601 movement type) according to consumption mode & consuption period maintained in MMRNA
50When sales order is enteredWhen delivery (PGI) is posted against the sale order (601 movement type) according to consumption mode & consuption period maintained in MMRNA

Factors affecting the Consumption and reduction

Both consumption and reduction are affected by the parameters like consumption mode and period in days maintained in the mrp 3 view of material master and it have the highest priority. If you do not specify a consumption mode, the system uses the consumption parameters from the MRP group that is assigned to the material. If a consumption mode is not specified there, the consumption mode is set to '1' by default (backward consumption only) with a (backward) consumption period of 999 days. This means that the consumption cannot be deactivated

PIR Consumption Example:

Consumption Mode (Back & Forward) and Period 100 days, Strategy 40:

Created a PIR of 10 PC.

PIR quantiy got consumed by new sales order of 1 pc and changed to 9 pc.

But in MD63 you can see that there are no changes as PIR remains 10 pc.

PIR Reduction Example:

Same material with same master data like consumption mode and period. But here I have created a delivery for the sales order and posted goods issue via VL01N. This reduced the PIR in MD63 itself.

MD04 after PGI

Some configurations that affect the consumption and reduction:

OVZG setting for req.class 030: Independent requirement reduction indicator set.

OPPS (Planning strategy 40)

OMJJ

Conclusion:

Please have a look on link to get idea on demand flow from sales order to production.

entry.title: 🚀 Steps to Perform Benchmarking Evaluation Using Generative AI Hub on BTP
entry.content:
Here’s a concise walkthrough for setting up and running a benchmarking evaluation using the Generative AI Hub service in SAP BTP:

1. Assign Role Collection for Generative AI Hub Access

  • To access the evaluation service under Generative AI Hub, assign the role collection ailaunchpad_genai_manager to the user of your BTP landscape.

  • Roll Collection in BTP

2. Set Up Object Store for Evaluation

  • For the benchmarking exercise, you need an object store.
  • In this example, we use AWS S3 as the object store type.

3. Create Object Store Secrets

  • Define two object store secrets in AICore:
    • One for input files (e.g., test datasets, orchestration config).
    • Another for evaluation results.
    • The secret for evaluation results must be named default.
    • The input file secret can be named according to your use case.

       

    • This maps to an S3 path such as:
      s3://hcp-5e9a9c35-3dd8-493a-bb55-7980c01fd279/td4
      And can be accessed using the alias:
      ai://err-res-genai-data.

4. Connect Local System to AWS S3 Object Store

  • Follow Step 9 from the official learning journey to connect your local environment to AWS S3 through BTP:
    👉SAP AI Core Data Management - Step 9

5. Upload Datasets to Object Store

  • Refer to Step 10 of the same tutorial to upload datasets like test data, orchestration configs, and custom metrics from your local system to the configured AWS S3 bucket.

6. Register Artifacts in AICore

  • Register all relevant artifacts (test datasets, orchestration config, custom metric files) in AICore.

  • Use the root directory name eval-data to register the artifcats, the sub-directory and its files are automatically registered.

  • Navigate to the Other Artifacts section in the AI Launchpad dashboard and use “Add” to register the path.

Example path:
ai://err-res-genai-data/eval-data

  • ai://err-res-genai-data – Refers to your object store secret key.

  • eval-data – Root directory where you will create subfolders like /runs and /testdata.

7. Create & Monitor Evaluation Run

  • After registering the artifacts, create your Evaluation via the AICore interface.

  • Monitor the execution workflow under the Executions tab to track progress and results.

  • The benchmarking metrics selected in Step 7 are evaluated based on the input artifacts and presented as follows.

entry.title: Leveraging Linear Regression in SAP AI: Predicting Business Outcomes with Data Science
entry.content:
1. Understanding Linear Regression in SAP AI
Linear regression is a widely used statistical method that helps forecast future values by analyzing historical data. In SAP AI, it is applied to predict business outcomes, optimize resources, and enhance enterprise-wide decision-making.

How Linear Regression Works in SAP:
The model is based on the following formula:

Y=β0+β1X+ε
Where:

Y = Predicted business metric (e.g., future sales, machine downtime)

X = Independent variable (e.g., historical transaction volume, sensor readings)

β0, β1 = Regression coefficients determined during model training

ε = Error term

SAP enables AI-driven linear regression through Predictive Analytics Library (PAL) in SAP HANA, offering businesses data-driven insights for more efficient decision-making.

2. Real-World Applications of Linear Regression in SAP AI
🔹 Predictive Maintenance in SAP S/4HANA
SAP AI applies linear regression to predict when an industrial machine will fail, allowing businesses to perform maintenance before breakdowns occur. ✅ Example: A manufacturing plant uses SAP S/4HANA and IoT sensor data to predict motor failure trends based on previous wear-and-tear cycles, preventing unplanned downtime.

🔹 Sales Forecasting in SAP Analytics Cloud
Linear regression helps forecast future revenue trends, allowing organizations to adjust marketing strategies and optimize inventory planning. ✅ Example: A retail company leverages SAP Analytics Cloud to predict the impact of seasonal discounts on quarterly revenue, ensuring optimal stock levels.

🔹 Demand Planning in SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning)
Using regression models, businesses can analyze demand fluctuations to align production schedules with consumer needs. ✅ Example: An automotive supplier predicts demand for specific car components based on historical orders, optimizing procurement and warehouse stock.

🔹 Customer Churn Prediction in SAP CRM
SAP AI uses historical customer data to identify patterns associated with churn, allowing companies to take proactive retention measures. ✅ Example: A telecom provider detects decreasing engagement levels in high-risk customers, triggering customized offers to retain loyalty.

🔹 Financial Risk Assessment in SAP HANA
Linear regression models assess credit risk and optimize financial decision-making for businesses and banks. ✅ Example: A bank running SAP HANA predicts loan default probabilities by analyzing a customer’s transaction history, spending behavior, and external economic factors.

🔹 Warehouse and Logistics Optimization in SAP EWM
By analyzing historical shipment data, regression models forecast delivery delays and enhance route planning. ✅ Example: A logistics company uses SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) to predict peak shipping congestion and allocate warehouse space accordingly.

🔹 Employee Attrition Prediction in SAP SuccessFactors
SAP AI can identify employees at risk of leaving, helping HR teams develop retention strategies. ✅ Example: A company uses linear regression models to assess work engagement metrics, determining the likelihood of turnover based on workload trends and satisfaction surveys.

3. Implementing Linear Regression in SAP AI Core & SAP AI Launchpad
SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad provide enterprise-grade AI capabilities that streamline the deployment and management of AI models.

🔹 SAP AI Core
SAP AI Core is a runtime environment designed for heavy-load AI processing. It enables businesses to: ✅ Train and deploy AI models efficiently using Kubernetes-based infrastructure. ✅ Scale AI workloads dynamically with built-in autoscaling. ✅ Integrate AI models seamlessly into SAP applications via API endpoints.

🔹 SAP AI Launchpad
SAP AI Launchpad simplifies AI lifecycle management, allowing businesses to: ✅ Centralize AI model administration across multiple runtime instances. ✅ Monitor AI model performance and optimize configurations. ✅ Manage generative AI prompts and experiment with LLM-based solutions.
erprise, linear regression will play an essential role in predictive analytics. Whether it’s optimizing system efficiency, forecasting revenue, or reducing operational risks, businesses can leverage AI-powered insights to stay ahead.

As I continue to explore AI applications in SAP landscapes, I look forward to driving innovation at the intersection of Data Science, AI, and Enterprise Systems. How do you see SAP AI transforming business operations?
Example Use Case:
A global manufacturing company uses SAP AI Core to train predictive maintenance models for SAP S/4HANA, while SAP AI Launchpad helps monitor AI model performance and streamline deployment across multiple business units.

4. The Future of AI in SAP With SAP evolving into an AI-driven 


With SAP evolving into an AI-driven enterprise, linear regression will play an essential role in predictive analytics. Whether it’s optimizing system efficiency, forecasting revenue, or reducing operational risks, businesses can leverage AI-powered insights to stay ahead.

As I continue to explore AI applications in SAP landscapes, I look forward to driving innovation at the intersection of Data Science, AI, and Enterprise Systems.

entry.title: How to Integrate AI Models into SAP BTP with Python and TensorFlow
entry.content:
As enterprises increasingly rely on data-driven decision-making, integrating custom AI models into business processes has moved from innovation to necessity. SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) empowers developers to build, deploy, and operationalize AI models directly into SAP workflows. This article offers a hands-on guide to integrating custom machine learning models built with Python and TensorFlow into SAP BTP, using SAP AI Core and AI API services.

Whether you're predicting sales, classifying documents, or automating business approvals, this guide will help you go from code to integration—step by step.

Prerequisites

Before diving in, ensure you have the following:

  • SAP BTP account with access to:
    • SAP AI Core
    • SAP AI API
    • SAP AI Launchpad (optional for lifecycle management)
  • Python 3.8+ and pip
  • TensorFlow (v2.x)
  • Docker (for containerization)
  • Basic understanding of ML model training and REST APIs

Step 1: Develop and Train a TensorFlow Model in Python

Let’s assume we are building a binary classification model that predicts customer churn.

import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

# Load sample data
df = pd.read_csv('customer_data.csv')
X = df.drop('churn', axis=1).values
y = df['churn'].values

# Split data
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.2)

# Define a simple model
model = Sequential([
Dense(64, activation='relu', input_shape=(X_train.shape[1],)),
Dense(1, activation='sigmoid')
])

model.compile(optimizer='adam', loss='binary_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])

# Train the model
model.fit(X_train, y_train, epochs=10, validation_data=(X_test, y_test))

# Save model
model.save('churn_model')

Step 2: Containerize the Model with Docker

You need to serve the model using a RESTful API via Flask or FastAPI and Dockerize the app.

app.py (Flask serving example)

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np

model = tf.keras.models.load_model('churn_model')

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/predict', methods=['POST'])
def predict():
data = request.get_json(force=True)
input_data = np.array(data['input']).reshape(1, -1)
prediction = model.predict(input_data)
return jsonify({'churn_probability': float(prediction[0][0])})

Dockerfile

FROM python:3.9-slim

WORKDIR /app
COPY . /app

RUN pip install flask tensorflow

EXPOSE 5000

CMD ["python", "app.py"]

Step 3: Register the Model in SAP AI Core

a) Define an AI Resource Configuration (ai-deployment.yaml)

apiVersion: ai.sap.com/v1alpha1
kind: ServingTemplate
metadata:
name: churn-predictor
spec:
image: yourusername/churn-model
protocol: rest
ports:
- port: 5000
resources:
limits:
cpu: "1"
memory: "1Gi"

Deploy this via the SAP AI Core CLI or Launchpad.

aicorectl apply -f ai-deployment.yaml

Step 4: Expose the Model with SAP AI API

After deployment, you can expose your model via the AI API by registering it under the respective AI Use Case.

Once registered, your service can be accessed at:

https://<your-instance>.ai.sap.com/inference/predict

Using curl or Postman, test your inference endpoint:

curl -X POST https://<your-instance>.ai.sap.com/inference/predict \
-H "Authorization: Bearer <access_token>" \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{"input": [0.5, 0.8, 0.3, 0.7]}'

Step 5: Integrate with SAP Applications

Now that your model is live, you can embed it into business workflows:

  • SAP S/4HANA: Use BTP extensions or SAP Workflow Service to call the AI API during sales, procurement, or finance processes.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud: Use the model for embedded predictions in dashboards.
  • SAP Intelligent RPA: Trigger predictions during robotic process automation flows.

Architecture Diagram

Here’s how the flow works:

+---------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+
| SAP Application | --> | SAP AI API | --> | Dockerized Model |
| (e.g., S/4HANA) | | (Inference Layer)| | on AI Core |
+---------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+
           ^                     |
           |                     v
   Business Logic           Token-based Auth

Best Practices

  • Security: Always use OAuth 2.0 tokens to secure endpoints.

  • Model Monitoring: Use AI Launchpad for versioning, monitoring, and lifecycle management.

  • Retraining: Automate retraining pipelines using SAP Data Intelligence or Apache Airflow on BTP.

SAP BTP allows you to operationalize custom AI models within the enterprise ecosystem—no need to wait for off-the-shelf integrations. By combining TensorFlow’s flexibility with SAP AI Core’s infrastructure, you can build robust, scalable, and secure AI-powered workflows.

entry.title: Central Job Scheduling - Successor for SAP Solution Manager?
entry.content:
Hello,

We are using SAP Solution Manager as a central job scheduling tool (BC-XBP scheduling jobs from Document) to schedule background jobs on our SAP systems. Support for Solution Manager ends by 2027 so we are looking for a successor tool or platform, which is able to easily document and schedule background jobs on managed systems, which are in Private Cloud (Rise with SAP)/onpremise. SAP Cloud ALM provides monitoring for jobs, but there is no job scheduler planned.

We have found some informations about this topic on SAP:

Question: Currently we use SAP Solution Manager and SAP BPA by Redwood for Job Management. However, according to SAP Note 3216013(opens in new tab) end of Mainstream Maintenance for SAP BPA by Redwood is 31.12.2024 and SAP Solution Manager maintenance will end 31.12.2027. Will SAP Cloud ALM offer a similar functionality?

Answer: SAP Cloud ALM offers standardized ALM functions focused on operational efficiency and is free of charge for SAP customers (see here). For process automation SAP offers SAP Build Process Automation, but this product is not a successor or replacement for SAP BPA by Redwood or another external job scheduling tool like Tivoli, which have a very strong focus and a broad range of features for efficient batch process (chain) execution. To replace an external scheduler, you need to look in detail at the features and functions to decide, if you can manage job scheduling as follows: 

  • use product inbuilt job scheduling tools (e.g. SAP Application Jobs in case of S/4HANA) for those processes, where SAP BPA by Redwood is used only for triggering batch execution 
  • use SAP Build Process Automation (or alternative SaaS automation tools) for those processes, where SAP BPA by Redwood is used in a complex way, i.e. not only triggering the execution of a job, but based on the result triggering follow on executions 
  • use Job & Automation Monitoring with SAP Cloud ALM that is retrieving job execution data directly from all SAP services and systems for monitoring.

The Job Scheduling functionality was included in SOLMAN and was pretty straightforward and easy to use. It feels like using SAP BPA or RunMyJobs from RedWood (which are both very complex and not included in cloud license) is not the best option. Are there any other options for central job scheduling? Is job scheduling really not planned for SAP Cloud ALM? 

Or is it possible to keep using SAP Solution Manager as a central Job Scheduling tool after 2027?

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Is Office 365 Web Version compatible with the SAP GUI...
entry.content:
Hello Almin

Refer to https://me.sap.com/notes/722513 

Microsoft Office 365 Online (only in a browser)

If your Office 365 license plan includes only Microsoft Office Online, you can work only with the online versions of Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, and OneNote.

These versions run in your browser and have restricted functions compared to locally installed versions of Office that are contained in your Office 365 license agreement (MSI or Click-To-Run). Below is a (non-exhaustive) list of these restrictions:

  • No support for macros in Office Documents (see http://office.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint-server-help/differences-between-using-a-workbook-in-the-browser-and-in-excel-HA010369179.aspx)
  • Various other functions are not supported: Mail merge, form fields, link server, and more (see http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/jj819298.aspx)

Since Desktop Office Integration makes extensive use of the above features, it cannot support Office 365 Online. Moreover, it is not possible to use the functions elsewhere. For this reason, it will not be possible to release Desktop Office Integration for Office 365 Online

entry.title: Combine cube's LEFT join returns no data when right side query returns no data: Why?
entry.content:
Environment:

  • SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform 4.3 Support Pack 4 Patch 8
  • Version : 14.3.4.5099.8
  • SUSE Linux
  • Accessing a SAP S4 Environment on Hana 2.0

Issue Summary:

  • Within SAP Business Objects, combine cube's LEFT join returns no data when the right side of the join query result is: "There is no dataset to display for this cube".

Issue Details: 

  • Working on creating a new report to display vendor data along with a "Last Activity Date" based on greatest of PO, GR, Invoice, Payment data. 
  • A The report has multiple queries returning data and at times no data in some of the queries exists.
    • Think "Vendor Data" (has data) and "Invoice Payments" (sometimes has data)
  • The report uses a combine cube (LEFT JOIN) to join the vendor data to the payment data based on the vendor ID. 
  • The report provides an optional prompt for the user to provide a "Vendor ID"
  • When the report is refreshed and the optional prompt is filled in; if a vendor has no payments issued; the combine cube results in no data even though it's a left join from vendor to payment data.  The vendor record exists; just no data is returned from payments query.   The result from the combine cube is 0 records. (how odd for a left join!) However, there is PO data and thus a last activity date exists; but all this gets wiped out since there is no payment data (yet)
  • When the optional prompt is left alone, it appears all data is returned as expected; and the vendor ID used for the Optional Prompt is included. 

Results:

  • EXPECTED: Since this is to behave like a SQL left join; I would have expected the vendor information to be displayed with no payment data (NULLs) 
  • ACTUAL: it behaves like an inner join and no data is returned in the combine cube since no data is returned in the payment data. 

Question:

  • Is this a bug or is this by design? If by design; where can I find documentation indicating this is how it is intended to work; because this is not how I would expect a SQL left join to function.  My next steps of opening an Influencer ticket or a Incident ticket depend on responses. 
  • Is anyone aware of this being patched in newer versions or is there a KBA already on this? 

Supporting Screenshots for Reference: 

  • Note in the below image; we join 4 queries together:
  • 1 row is returned for the vendor master since we selected a single vendor master on our prompt.
  • multiple payment dates (4) are returned; and result is left joined to vendor master
  • 0 change log records are returned; and result is left joined to vendor master
  • 0 Latest Activity records are returned; and result is left joined to vendor master
  • Note the result of the combined cube is 0 records when I would expect a minimum of 1 if no data in last payment date; but 4 since there were 4 past payment dates.  Why is the combine cube resulting in 0?

Any insights or thoughts would be appreciated.  its operating as if the queries with no records do not produce files on the BO server for the combine cube to use.  This appears to result the entire combine cube failing; which is unexpected behavior to me.

entry.title: How to force password reset?
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

How to force a user to reset their password?

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to force password reset?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

SAP Customer Data Cloud supports an operation that allows a site's administrator to manually expire a user password such that the user will be prompted to set a new password upon their next login. This can be done by calling the "accounts.setAccountInfo" REST API method and passing the "requirePasswordChange" parameter as "true".

Please note the following:

  • it is necessary to call this for each user that you want to manually expire their password
  • it is important to ensure that a "data-on-pending-password-change-screen" is correctly mapped on the login screen-set so that the user will be able to complete this flow (in the UI Builder the default screen used for this is the "Mandatory Pwd Change" screen)

 

Best Regards,

SAP Solution Support
